ItemDataXML.Name: None
ItemDataXML.Desc: Empty Space

ItemDataXML.Name: Grass
ItemDataXML.Desc: Grass covered dirt. Usually found on the surface.

ItemDataXML.Name: Dirt
ItemDataXML.Desc: Dirt is a natural body composed of particles of broken rock that have been altered by chemical and environmental processes that include weathering and erosion.

ItemDataXML.Name: Sand
ItemDataXML.Desc: Sand is a naturally occurring granular material composed of finely divided rock and mineral particles. The most common constituent of sand in inland continental settings and non-tropical coastal settings is silica, usually in the form of quartz.

ItemDataXML.Name: Scoria
ItemDataXML.Desc: Scoria is a porous volcanic rock that has high surface area and strength for its weight, and often striking colours. Scoria is often used in landscaping and drainage works. It is also commonly used in barbeque grills.

ItemDataXML.Name: Wood
ItemDataXML.Desc: Trees grow anywhere there is dirt and space. Chop trees down and craft the wood into wood planks. Trees may drop Saplings when chopped down. You can farm trees by planting Saplings.

ItemDataXML.Name: Wood Plank
ItemDataXML.Desc: Wood planks are crafted from tree wood blocks. Wood planks are in turn used to craft many other items.

ItemDataXML.Name: Wood Veneer
ItemDataXML.Desc: A decorative wood veneer used to furnish many types of wooden furniture.

ItemDataXML.Name: Leaves
ItemDataXML.Desc: Leaves grow on trees, unlike money. Leaves will die if their tree is chopped down.

ItemDataXML.Name: Glass
ItemDataXML.Desc: Glass is solid but see-through. Useful for windows... Made from sand in a furnace.

ItemDataXML.Name: Cloud
ItemDataXML.Desc: This is not an internet based digital storage block. It is a traditional cloud, a visible mass of liquid droplets or frozen crystals made of water or various chemicals suspended in the atmosphere above the surface. You know, "I can see clearly now the Cloud has gone."

ItemDataXML.Name: Water
ItemDataXML.Desc: Water is wet. Splash Splash.

ItemDataXML.Name: Copper
ItemDataXML.Desc: Copper is a ductile metal with very high thermal and electrical conductivity. It is used as a conductor of heat and electricity, a building material, and a constituent of various metal alloys. Bronze is a combination of Copper and Tin.

ItemDataXML.Name: Lava
ItemDataXML.Desc: Ouch.

ItemDataXML.Name: Cassiterite
ItemDataXML.Desc: Cassiterite is a tin oxide mineral. It is generally opaque, but it is translucent in thin crystals. It's luster and multiple crystal faces produce a desirable gem. Cassiterite has been the chief tin ore throughout ancient history and remains the most important source of tin today.

ItemDataXML.Name: Clay
ItemDataXML.Desc: Clay is a naturally occurring aluminium silicate composed primarily of fine-grained minerals. Clay is often put into a furnace to make bricks and pottery.

ItemDataXML.Name: Sandstone
ItemDataXML.Desc: Sandstone is a sedimentary rock composed mainly of sand-sized minerals or rock grains. Most sandstone is composed of quartz and/or feldspar because these are the most common minerals in the Earth's crust.

ItemDataXML.Name: Limestone
ItemDataXML.Desc: Limestone is a sedimentary rock composed largely of the carbon crystal minerals calcite and/or aragonite.

ItemDataXML.Name: Basalt
ItemDataXML.Desc: Basalt is a common extrusive volcanic rock. It is one of the most common rock types in the world.

ItemDataXML.Name: Andesite
ItemDataXML.Desc: Andesite is an extrusive igneous, volcanic rock, of intermediate composition, with aphanitic to porphyritic texture. In a general sense, it is the intermediate type between basalt and dacite.

ItemDataXML.Name: Dacite
ItemDataXML.Desc: Dacite is an igneous, volcanic rock. It is intermediate in compositions between andesite and rhyolite. The word dacite comes from Dacia, a province of the Roman Empire which lay between the Danube River and Carpathian Mountains (now modern Romania) where the rock was first described.

ItemDataXML.Name: Diorite
ItemDataXML.Desc: Diorite is a grey to dark grey intermediate intrusive igneous rock. Diorite is a relatively rare rock. It is an extremely hard rock, making it difficult to carve and work with. It is so hard that ancient civilizations (such as Ancient Egypt) used diorite balls to work granite.

ItemDataXML.Name: Tuff
ItemDataXML.Desc: Tuff is closely associated with volcanoes. It tends to form when erupting lavas are stiff and high in silica, which holds the volcanic gases in bubbles rather than letting it escape. Tuff is a rock of great variety and tells the geologist a lot about conditions during the eruptions that gave birth to it.

ItemDataXML.Name: Serpentine
ItemDataXML.Desc: Serpentine is a rock composed of one or more serpentine group minerals. Minerals in this group are formed by serpentinization, a hydration and metamorphic transformation of ultramafic rock from the Earth's mantle.

ItemDataXML.Name: Gabbro
ItemDataXML.Desc: Gabbro is a dark plutonic rock that is considered to be the plutonic equivalent of basalt. Unlike granite, gabbro is low in silica and has no quartz.

ItemDataXML.Name: Granite
ItemDataXML.Desc: Granite is a common and widely occurring type of intrusive igneous rock. Granite is nearly always massive (lacking internal structures), hard and tough, and therefore it has gained widespread use as a construction stone.

ItemDataXML.Name: Komatiite
ItemDataXML.Desc: Komatiite is a type of ultramafic mantle-derived volcanic rock. True komatiites are very rare and essentially restricted to rocks of Archaean age. Komatiite magma is extremely dense and unlikely to reach the surface, being more likely to pool lower within the crust.

ItemDataXML.Name: Marble
ItemDataXML.Desc: Marble is a metamorphic rock composed of carbonate minerals, most commonly calcite or dolomite. Marble is commonly used for sculpture and as a building material.

ItemDataXML.Name: Rhyolite
ItemDataXML.Desc: Rhyolite can be considered as the extrusive equivalent to the plutonic granite rock, and outcrops of rhyolite may bear a resemblance to granite. Rhyolites that cool too quickly to grow crystals form a natural glass or vitrophyre, also called obsidian.

ItemDataXML.Name: Bedrock
ItemDataXML.Desc: Bedrock. Congratulations you've made it to the bottom of the world!

ItemDataXML.Name: Carbon
ItemDataXML.Desc: Carbon is the 15th most abundant element in the Earth's crust, and the fourth most abundant element in the universe by mass after hydrogen, helium, and oxygen. Carbon can combined with other ores to form alloys.

ItemDataXML.Name: Obsidian
ItemDataXML.Desc: Obsidian is a naturally occurring volcanic glass formed as an extrusive igneous rock. It has magical properties. Teleportation has been created using Obsidian blocks.

ItemDataXML.Name: Coal
ItemDataXML.Desc: Coal is a combustible black or brownish-black sedimentary rock normally occurring in rock strata in layers or veins called coal beds or coal seams. Coal begins as layers of plant matter accumulate at the bottom of a body of water. Coal, a fossil fuel, is the largest source of energy for the generation of electricity worldwide. It is also a good fuel for furnaces.

ItemDataXML.Name: Flint
ItemDataXML.Desc: Flint is a hard, sedimentary cryptocrystalline form of the mineral quartz. Flint was used for the manufacture of flint tools during the Stone Age as it splits into thin, sharp splinters called flakes or blades. When struck against steel, a flint edge will produce sparks.

ItemDataXML.Name: Greenstone
ItemDataXML.Desc: Greenstone is several types of hard, durable and highly valued nephrite jade and bowenite found in New Zealand. It is used to make jewelry and weapons in the form of clubs.

ItemDataXML.Name: Diamond
ItemDataXML.Desc: This rock contains diamond. Diamond gemstones can be extracted from this rock by smelting it in a furnace. Diamonds are the hardest naturally occuring gemstones in the world, and can make some nice tools.

ItemDataXML.Name: Ruby
ItemDataXML.Desc: This rock contains ruby. Ruby gemstones can be extracted from this rock by smelting it in a furnace. Rubies can add a bit of oomph into your diamond tools.

ItemDataXML.Name: Gold
ItemDataXML.Desc: Gold is a dense, soft, shiny metal and the most malleable and ductile metal known. Pure gold has a bright yellow color and luster traditionally considered attractive, which it maintains without oxidizing in air or water. With exception of the noble gases, gold is the least reactive chemical element known. It has been a valuable and highly sought-after precious metal for coinage, jewelry, and other arts since long before the beginning of recorded history.

ItemDataXML.Name: Platinum
ItemDataXML.Desc: Platinum's name is derived from the Spanish term platina del Pinto, which is literally translated into 'little silver of the Pinto River.' It is a dense, malleable, ductile, precious, gray-white and extremely rare transition metal. Platinum is one of the rarest elements in the Earth's crust.

ItemDataXML.Name: Iron
ItemDataXML.Desc: Iron is the most common element on planet Earth, forming much of Earth's outer and inner core, and it is the fourth most common element in the Earth's crust. Iron is the sixth most common element in the Universe.

ItemDataXML.Name: Opal
ItemDataXML.Desc: Opal is an amorphous form of silica related to quartz. It is deposited at a relatively low temperature and may occur in the fissures of almost any kind of rock, being most commonly found with limestone, sandstone, rhyolite, marble and basalt.

ItemDataXML.Name: Uranium
ItemDataXML.Desc: Uranium is a silvery-white metallic chemical element in the actinide series of the periodic table with atomic number 92. It is assigned the chemical symbol U.

ItemDataXML.Name: Cobblestone
ItemDataXML.Desc: Cobblestone is a versatile building material. Crafted from Basalt. Cobblestone often collects moss, so clean it regularly.

ItemDataXML.Name: Bricks
ItemDataXML.Desc: Bricks are a popular and versatile building material. Fashioned from clay in a furnace.

ItemDataXML.Name: Chess
ItemDataXML.Desc: Chess blocks are used for construction and decoration. If you get one of your pawns to the other side of the board, you can get another little horsey!

ItemDataXML.Name: Bookcase
ItemDataXML.Desc: Store your books, blueprints and scrolls in bookcases, or just put bookcases around your house to decorate and appear learned.

ItemDataXML.Name: Torch
ItemDataXML.Desc: Light up those dark places with torches. Torches do not burn out.

ItemDataXML.Name: Ladder
ItemDataXML.Desc: Use ladders to access vertical tunnels or levels in buildings.

ItemDataXML.Name: Workbench
ItemDataXML.Desc: Use a workbench to craft items that require 3 x 3 materials.

ItemDataXML.Name: Furnace
ItemDataXML.Desc: Use furnaces to smelt a variety of items into other forms.

ItemDataXML.Name: Chest
ItemDataXML.Desc: Use chests to store your items. Items remain in chests permanently, until removed.

ItemDataXML.Name: Wood Door
ItemDataXML.Desc: Open... Close... Open... Close... Open... Close...

ItemDataXML.Name: Steel Door
ItemDataXML.Desc: Open... Close... Open... Close... Open... Close... A sturdy steel door with blast resistance.

ItemDataXML.Name: Teleport
ItemDataXML.Desc: Oooo the pretty lights.

ItemDataXML.Name: TNT
ItemDataXML.Desc: TNT (Trinitrotoluene) is one of the most commonly used explosives for military and industrial applications. It's insensitivity to shock and friction reduces the risk of accidental detonation. TNT neither absorbs nor dissolves in water, which allows it to be used effectively in wet environments. Additionally, it is stable compared to other high explosives. Torches and grenades have been known to detonate TNT.

ItemDataXML.Name: C4
ItemDataXML.Desc: As with many plastic explosives, the explosive in C4 is RDX (cyclonite or cyclotrimethylene trinitramine). A major advantage of C4 is that it can easily be molded into any desired shape. C4 is very stable and insensitive to most physical shocks. Detonation can only be initiated by a combination of extreme heat and a shock wave, as when a detonator inserted into it is fired. Torches and grenades have been known to detonate C4.

ItemDataXML.Name: Wisdom
ItemDataXML.Desc: A Scroll of Mythological Wisdom.

ItemDataXML.Name: Blueprint
ItemDataXML.Desc: Blueprints give you the information you need to craft items. You must have an item blueprint before you can craft that item. Shop items are locked until you find their blueprint.

ItemDataXML.Name: Sapling
ItemDataXML.Desc: Saplings drop from trees when chopped. They will grow into a tree under suitable conditions.

ItemDataXML.Name: White Flowers
ItemDataXML.Desc: Pretty white flowers. Used to decorate gardens, flower beds, and pretty much anywhere else.

ItemDataXML.Name: Purple Flowers
ItemDataXML.Desc: Pretty purple flowers. Used to decorate gardens, flower beds, and pretty much anywhere else.

ItemDataXML.Name: Red Flowers
ItemDataXML.Desc: Pretty red flowers. Used to decorate gardens, flower beds, and pretty much anywhere else.

ItemDataXML.Name: Yellow Flowers
ItemDataXML.Desc: Pretty yellow flowers. Used to decorate gardens, flower beds, and pretty much anywhere else.

ItemDataXML.Name: Pine Leaves
ItemDataXML.Desc: Leaves grow on trees, unlike money. Leaves will die if their tree is chopped down.

ItemDataXML.Name: Item Shop
ItemDataXML.Desc: Shop till you drop! Shops can be transported around the surface but cannot be taken deep underground.

ItemDataXML.Name: Block Shop
ItemDataXML.Desc: Best prices in town! Shops can be transported around the surface but cannot be taken deep underground.

ItemDataXML.Name: Sulphur
ItemDataXML.Desc: Sulfur crystals are commonly sought after by mineral collectors for their brightly colored polyhedron shapes. Sulfur is referenced in the Bible as brimstone. It was needed to make black gunpowder.

ItemDataXML.Name: Cyclonite
ItemDataXML.Desc: Cyclonite is an explosive nitroamine widely used in military and industrial applications. As an explosive, it is usually used in mixtures with other explosives and plasticizers, phlegmatizers or desensitizers. It is stable in storage and is considered one of the most powerful and brisant of the military high explosives.

ItemDataXML.Name: Titanium
ItemDataXML.Desc: Applications for titanium can be found in industrial, aerospace, recreational, and emerging markets. Powdered titanium is used in pyrotechnics as a source of bright-burning particles.

ItemDataXML.Name: Teflon
ItemDataXML.Desc: Teflon is a utility block that will slide to the ground if no other blocks are underneath it.

ItemDataXML.Name: Fluorite
ItemDataXML.Desc: Fluorite is a colorful mineral, both in visible light and often under ultraviolet, and the stone has ornamental and lapidary uses. Fluorite is a mineral form of Fluorine.

ItemDataXML.Name: Woven Leaves
ItemDataXML.Desc: Leaves woven into a material suitable for crafting other items, or just for decoration.

ItemDataXML.Name: Rope
ItemDataXML.Desc: Rope is more convenient than ladders for deep shafts. Place rope at the top, and it will uncoil downwards until it hits something or it's length runs out. Rope ascend and descend speed is quicker than ladders. Rope is also ideal to descend open spaces as it does not need to be attached to a solid block.

ItemDataXML.Name: Multi-Texture Block
ItemDataXML.Desc: This is a standard block but it allows you to change it's material texture.

ItemDataXML.Name: Mossy Cobblestone
ItemDataXML.Desc: Mossy Cobblestone is a building material that gives the look of age and disrepair.

ItemDataXML.Name: Pane
ItemDataXML.Desc: A thin, decorative block, mainly used for thin walls or windows.

ItemDataXML.Name: Stained Glass Pane
ItemDataXML.Desc: Beautiful colored glass panes.

ItemDataXML.Name: Post
ItemDataXML.Desc: A thick post.

ItemDataXML.Name: Health Block
ItemDataXML.Desc: A Combat dummy. Set the health, then unleash your weapons on the block as if it were your worst enemy.

ItemDataXML.Name: Grassy Stone
ItemDataXML.Desc: Large flat stones set in grass. Naturally occuring, they can be used for pavements and decoration.

ItemDataXML.Name: Blue Box
ItemDataXML.Desc: A blue box! Not much more to say really. Used in the blueprint finder model.

ItemDataXML.Name: CustomBlock1
ItemDataXML.Desc: CustomBlock

ItemDataXML.Name: CustomBlock2
ItemDataXML.Desc: CustomBlock

ItemDataXML.Name: CustomBlock3
ItemDataXML.Desc: CustomBlock

ItemDataXML.Name: CustomBlock4
ItemDataXML.Desc: CustomBlock

ItemDataXML.Name: CustomBlock5
ItemDataXML.Desc: CustomBlock

ItemDataXML.Name: CustomBlock6
ItemDataXML.Desc: CustomBlock

ItemDataXML.Name: CustomBlock7
ItemDataXML.Desc: CustomBlock

ItemDataXML.Name: CustomBlock8
ItemDataXML.Desc: CustomBlock

ItemDataXML.Name: CustomBlock9
ItemDataXML.Desc: CustomBlock

ItemDataXML.Name: CustomBlock10
ItemDataXML.Desc: CustomBlock

ItemDataXML.Name: UserDefined1
ItemDataXML.Desc: UserDefined

ItemDataXML.Name: UserDefined2
ItemDataXML.Desc: UserDefined

ItemDataXML.Name: UserDefined3
ItemDataXML.Desc: UserDefined

ItemDataXML.Name: UserDefined4
ItemDataXML.Desc: UserDefined

ItemDataXML.Name: UserDefined5
ItemDataXML.Desc: UserDefined

ItemDataXML.Name: UserDefined6
ItemDataXML.Desc: UserDefined

ItemDataXML.Name: UserDefined7
ItemDataXML.Desc: UserDefined

ItemDataXML.Name: UserDefined8
ItemDataXML.Desc: UserDefined

ItemDataXML.Name: UserDefined9
ItemDataXML.Desc: UserDefined

ItemDataXML.Name: Void Marker
ItemDataXML.Desc: A creative tool that replaces itself with air.

ItemDataXML.Name: Rasta
ItemDataXML.Desc: The colors used in this block have a lot of deep connections and meanings that I don't have time to get into. But it's very interesting. I promise.

ItemDataXML.Name: White Tile
ItemDataXML.Desc: White Tile is a simple white tiled block used for construction and decoration.

ItemDataXML.Name: Checkered
ItemDataXML.Desc: Checkered block used for construction and decoration. Now, if I play right, I can take those two pieces there, then on my next turn...

ItemDataXML.Name: Retro
ItemDataXML.Desc: Life isn't always all sunshine and rainbows, but with this block it can be. Well, at least the rainbows part.

ItemDataXML.Name: Cherry Metal
ItemDataXML.Desc: Cherry Metal is a block used for construction and decoration. It's not actually made of cherries. You can't eat it. Believe me, I tried.

ItemDataXML.Name: Static
ItemDataXML.Desc: Static block used for construction and decoration. If you stare into it long enough, you can start to see the secrets of the universe... or, maybe it's just messing with my eyes.

ItemDataXML.Name: Ice
ItemDataXML.Desc: Ice. Frozen water, no Splash Splash.

ItemDataXML.Name: Concrete Brick
ItemDataXML.Desc: A versatile brick made from concrete. Most often used for construction.

ItemDataXML.Name: Turkish
ItemDataXML.Desc: Exquisitely crafted turkish carpet. Looks good on floors.

ItemDataXML.Name: Camouflage
ItemDataXML.Desc: Military style camouflage used for hiding things.

ItemDataXML.Name: Spider Egg
ItemDataXML.Desc: A rare item, collected by the brave. Place it on the ground at your own risk!

ItemDataXML.Name: Long Grass
ItemDataXML.Desc: Long uncultivated grass. Will sometimes yield seeds when cut.

ItemDataXML.Name: Stairs
ItemDataXML.Desc: Up... Down... Up... Down... Up... Down...

ItemDataXML.Name: Ambient Sound Block
ItemDataXML.Desc: A block used to create audio ambience.

ItemDataXML.Name: Wood Door
ItemDataXML.Desc: Open... Close... Open... Close... Open... Close...

ItemDataXML.Name: Steel Door
ItemDataXML.Desc: Open... Close... Open... Close... Open... Close... A sturdy steel door with blast resistance.

ItemDataXML.Name: Sign
ItemDataXML.Desc: Don't you just hate all those advertisements?

ItemDataXML.Name: Fire
ItemDataXML.Desc: When man learned to control fire, things changed.

ItemDataXML.Name: Steel Spikes
ItemDataXML.Desc: Rusty, Bloody, Deadly Spikes! Watch your step!

ItemDataXML.Name: Climbing Ivy
ItemDataXML.Desc: Ivy attaches to vertical surfaces. You can climb surfaces covered in Ivy.

ItemDataXML.Name: Book
ItemDataXML.Desc: What's your story?

ItemDataXML.Name: Key
ItemDataXML.Desc: Keys are used to open locked things.

ItemDataXML.Name: Rares Chest
ItemDataXML.Desc: Contains extremely rare items.

ItemDataXML.Name: Crop
ItemDataXML.Desc: When man learned to farm, things changed.

ItemDataXML.Name: Invisible Barrier
ItemDataXML.Desc: This block is invisible and can be used as a see through barrier.

ItemDataXML.Name: Cover Block
ItemDataXML.Desc: This block imitates the block below it, but with a cover of some material.

ItemDataXML.Name: Red Mushroom
ItemDataXML.Desc: Red Mushrooms are tasty! But are they healthy?

ItemDataXML.Name: Arcade Machine
ItemDataXML.Desc: Arcade Games to bring back the good old times.

ItemDataXML.Name: Particle Emitter
ItemDataXML.Desc: An advanced block used create particles. Particles can do many things, such as adding animated parts to objects or creating ambient details that really tie a scene together.

ItemDataXML.Name: Stack
ItemDataXML.Desc: A stackable block.

ItemDataXML.Name: Upside Down Stack
ItemDataXML.Desc: An upside down stackable block.

ItemDataXML.Name: Locked Chest
ItemDataXML.Desc: Locked Chests can only be opened by the player that placed them.

ItemDataXML.Name: Lit Furnace
ItemDataXML.Desc: Use furnaces to smelt a variety of items into other forms. This furnace is fueled and hot!

ItemDataXML.Name: White Wool
ItemDataXML.Desc: Wool is useful for making other items.

ItemDataXML.Name: Bed Head
ItemDataXML.Desc: ZZZZZZzzzzzzzzzz. Sleep tight, don't let the bed bugs bite. If you're afraid of night, beds will help your plight.

ItemDataXML.Name: Bed Foot
ItemDataXML.Desc: ZZZZZZzzzzzzzzzz. Sleep tight, don't let the bed bugs bite. If you're afraid of night, beds will help your plight.

ItemDataXML.Name: NPC Spawn
ItemDataXML.Desc: Place these blocks to spawn Non Player Characters.

ItemDataXML.Name: Crate
ItemDataXML.Desc: Use crates to store free items that anybody can access. Items remain in crates permanently, until removed.

ItemDataXML.Name: Fence
ItemDataXML.Desc: What a lovely fence! Let's keep the riff-raff out!

ItemDataXML.Name: Locked Door
ItemDataXML.Desc: Open... Close... Open... Close... Open... Close... Oh wait, it's locked!

ItemDataXML.Name: Sun Box
ItemDataXML.Desc: A beautiful light box that shines like the sun.

ItemDataXML.Name: Sentry Turret
ItemDataXML.Desc: Pin those enemies back! Sentry turrets must have both a weapon and appropriate ammunition to function. Sentry turrets will not fire if the game is set to Peaceful Mode or Combat is disabled.

ItemDataXML.Name: Proximity Detector
ItemDataXML.Desc: Emits power to adjacent blocks when players or mobs come within range.

ItemDataXML.Name: Snow
ItemDataXML.Desc: Where's that snowboard gone?

ItemDataXML.Name: Snow Layer
ItemDataXML.Desc: Where's that snowboard gone?

ItemDataXML.Name: Scaffold
ItemDataXML.Desc: Climbable scaffolding made of wood and steel.

ItemDataXML.Name: Steel Portcullis
ItemDataXML.Desc: A fortified glass portal.

ItemDataXML.Name: Steel Plating
ItemDataXML.Desc: Solid Steel Plating. Can give an industrial look to any build it's added to.

ItemDataXML.Name: Half Block
ItemDataXML.Desc: Half of a block.

ItemDataXML.Name: Ramp
ItemDataXML.Desc: A sloped ramp block.

ItemDataXML.Name: Cactus
ItemDataXML.Desc: Prickly Juicy Goodness.

ItemDataXML.Name: Terracotta Tile
ItemDataXML.Desc: Ceramic shingles that would fit perfectly on a roof.

ItemDataXML.Name: One Way Glass
ItemDataXML.Desc: One way glass is see-through except for one side.

ItemDataXML.Name: Cylinder
ItemDataXML.Desc: A cylinder shaped block. Is it really a block at that point?

ItemDataXML.Name: Gold Block
ItemDataXML.Desc: A block of solid gold. It is smelted from gold bars, and can be smelted back to gold bars. A good way to efficiently store gold.

ItemDataXML.Name: Stone Wall
ItemDataXML.Desc: A pretty stone wall.

ItemDataXML.Name: Sand Brick
ItemDataXML.Desc: Bricks made from sand.

ItemDataXML.Name: Table
ItemDataXML.Desc: A useful indoor decoration. Now, go set the table, we're expecting guests over for dinner tonight.

ItemDataXML.Name: Gilded Wood Panel
ItemDataXML.Desc: A beautifully decorated wood veneer panel with a gold trim.

ItemDataXML.Name: Painting
ItemDataXML.Desc: Wall Decoration. Used to display photos taken by a Camera.

ItemDataXML.Name: Grass Shaded
ItemDataXML.Desc: Shaded grass is usually found under tree foilage.

ItemDataXML.Name: Maple Leaves
ItemDataXML.Desc: Leaves grow on trees, unlike money. Leaves will die if their tree is chopped down.

ItemDataXML.Name: Wifi Transmitter
ItemDataXML.Desc: Used to transmit a power on or off signal to WifiReceivers.

ItemDataXML.Name: Wifi Receiver
ItemDataXML.Desc: Used to receive a power signal transmitted by WifiTransmitters. This receiver will either power adjacent blocks on or off, depending on the signal type.

ItemDataXML.Name: Pressure Plate
ItemDataXML.Desc: Watch your step, you might trigger something unpleasant. Then again, you might just open a door.

ItemDataXML.Name: Powered Light
ItemDataXML.Desc: A light that needs power to shine. Where's the light switch!

ItemDataXML.Name: Safe
ItemDataXML.Desc: For storing those valuables. Safes can only be opened if powered.

ItemDataXML.Name: Sundial
ItemDataXML.Desc: Used for calculating the time of day.

ItemDataXML.Name: Switch
ItemDataXML.Desc: Go on, flick the switch. What will happen? Will the lights come on? Will the door open? Or will you fall in to a nice pit of lava.

ItemDataXML.Name: Button
ItemDataXML.Desc: A button is like a switch, except it turns itself off after a short time.

ItemDataXML.Name: Trap Door
ItemDataXML.Desc: The classic trap. Opens when powered.

ItemDataXML.Name: Script Block
ItemDataXML.Desc: Runs scripts when powered on or off.

ItemDataXML.Name: Tilled Earth
ItemDataXML.Desc: Needed for farming. Seeds will only grow on irrigated tilled earth. If tilled earth is within four blocks of water and not below the water, it is considered irrigated.

ItemDataXML.Name: Salt Block
ItemDataXML.Desc: Natural salt.

ItemDataXML.Name: Entity Spawn
ItemDataXML.Desc: Use these blocks to spawn entities

ItemDataXML.Name: Bone
ItemDataXML.Desc: Bone from a dead animal.

ItemDataXML.Name: Sapphire
ItemDataXML.Desc: This rock contains Sapphire. Sapphire gemstones can be extracted from this rock by smelting it in a furnace. Sapphires make great jewelry jewels.

ItemDataXML.Name: Stone Brick
ItemDataXML.Desc: A brick made from stone. Most often used for construction.

ItemDataXML.Name: Birch Wood
ItemDataXML.Desc: Birch trees grow anywhere there is dirt and space. Chop Birch trees down and craft the Birch wood into Birch wood planks. Birch trees may drop Saplings when chopped down. You can farm trees by planting Saplings.

ItemDataXML.Name: Birch Wood Plank
ItemDataXML.Desc: Birch Wood planks are crafted from Birch tree wood blocks.

ItemDataXML.Name: Stained Glass
ItemDataXML.Desc: Beautiful colored glass.

ItemDataXML.Name: Unused1
ItemDataXML.Desc: Unused

ItemDataXML.Name: Unused2
ItemDataXML.Desc: Unused

ItemDataXML.Name: Unused3
ItemDataXML.Desc: Unused

ItemDataXML.Name: Unused4
ItemDataXML.Desc: Unused

ItemDataXML.Name: Unused5
ItemDataXML.Desc: Unused

ItemDataXML.Name: Side Post
ItemDataXML.Desc: A sideways post.

ItemDataXML.Name: Unused6
ItemDataXML.Desc: Unused

ItemDataXML.Name: Corner Block
ItemDataXML.Desc: A corner snugglying block.

ItemDataXML.Name: Unused7
ItemDataXML.Desc: Unused

ItemDataXML.Name: Unused8
ItemDataXML.Desc: Unused

ItemDataXML.Name: Polished Basalt
ItemDataXML.Desc: Basalt is a common extrusive volcanic rock. It is one of the most common rock types in the world.

ItemDataXML.Name: Polished Andesite
ItemDataXML.Desc: Andesite is an extrusive igneous, volcanic rock, of intermediate composition, with aphanitic to porphyritic texture. In a general sense, it is the intermediate type between basalt and dacite.

ItemDataXML.Name: Polished Dacite
ItemDataXML.Desc: Dacite is an igneous, volcanic rock. It is intermediate in compositions between andesite and rhyolite. The word dacite comes from Dacia, a province of the Roman Empire which lay between the Danube River and Carpathian Mountains (now modern Romania) where the rock was first described.

ItemDataXML.Name: Polished Diorite
ItemDataXML.Desc: Diorite is a grey to dark grey intermediate intrusive igneous rock. Diorite is a relatively rare rock. It is an extremely hard rock, making it difficult to carve and work with. It is so hard that ancient civilizations (such as Ancient Egypt) used diorite balls to work granite.

ItemDataXML.Name: Polished Tuff
ItemDataXML.Desc: Tuff is closely associated with volcanoes. It tends to form when erupting lavas are stiff and high in silica, which holds the volcanic gases in bubbles rather than letting it escape. Tuff is a rock of great variety and tells the geologist a lot about conditions during the eruptions that gave birth to it.

ItemDataXML.Name: Polished Serpentine
ItemDataXML.Desc: Serpentine is a rock composed of one or more serpentine group minerals. Minerals in this group are formed by serpentinization, a hydration and metamorphic transformation of ultramafic rock from the Earth's mantle.

ItemDataXML.Name: Polished Gabbro
ItemDataXML.Desc: Gabbro is a dark plutonic rock that is considered to be the plutonic equivalent of basalt. Unlike granite, gabbro is low in silica and has no quartz.

ItemDataXML.Name: Polished Granite
ItemDataXML.Desc: Granite is a common and widely occurring type of intrusive igneous rock. Granite is nearly always massive (lacking internal structures), hard and tough, and therefore it has gained widespread use as a construction stone.

ItemDataXML.Name: Polished Komatiite
ItemDataXML.Desc: Komatiite is a type of ultramafic mantle-derived volcanic rock. True komatiites are very rare and essentially restricted to rocks of Archaean age. Komatiite magma is extremely dense and unlikely to reach the surface, being more likely to pool lower within the crust.

ItemDataXML.Name: Polished Marble
ItemDataXML.Desc: Marble is a metamorphic rock composed of carbonate minerals, most commonly calcite or dolomite. Marble is commonly used for sculpture and as a building material.

ItemDataXML.Name: Polished Rhyolite
ItemDataXML.Desc: Rhyolite can be considered as the extrusive equivalent to the plutonic granite rock, and outcrops of rhyolite may bear a resemblance to granite. Rhyolites that cool too quickly to grow crystals form a natural glass or vitrophyre, also called obsidian.

ItemDataXML.Name: Polished Greenstone
ItemDataXML.Desc: Greenstone is several types of hard, durable and highly valued nephrite jade and bowenite found in New Zealand. It is used to make jewelry and weapons in the form of clubs.

ItemDataXML.Name: Locked Door
ItemDataXML.Desc: Open... Close... Open... Close... Open... Close... Oh wait, it's locked!

ItemDataXML.Name: Polished Basalt Brick
ItemDataXML.Desc: Basalt is a common extrusive volcanic rock. It is one of the most common rock types in the world.

ItemDataXML.Name: Polished Andesite Brick
ItemDataXML.Desc: Andesite is an extrusive igneous, volcanic rock, of intermediate composition, with aphanitic to porphyritic texture. In a general sense, it is the intermediate type between basalt and dacite.

ItemDataXML.Name: Polished Dacite Brick
ItemDataXML.Desc: Dacite is an igneous, volcanic rock. It is intermediate in compositions between andesite and rhyolite. The word dacite comes from Dacia, a province of the Roman Empire which lay between the Danube River and Carpathian Mountains (now modern Romania) where the rock was first described.

ItemDataXML.Name: Polished Diorite Brick
ItemDataXML.Desc: Diorite is a grey to dark grey intermediate intrusive igneous rock. Diorite is a relatively rare rock. It is an extremely hard rock, making it difficult to carve and work with. It is so hard that ancient civilizations (such as Ancient Egypt) used diorite balls to work granite.

ItemDataXML.Name: Polished Tuff Brick
ItemDataXML.Desc: Tuff is closely associated with volcanoes. It tends to form when erupting lavas are stiff and high in silica, which holds the volcanic gases in bubbles rather than letting it escape. Tuff is a rock of great variety and tells the geologist a lot about conditions during the eruptions that gave birth to it.

ItemDataXML.Name: Polished Serpentine Brick
ItemDataXML.Desc: Serpentine is a rock composed of one or more serpentine group minerals. Minerals in this group are formed by serpentinization, a hydration and metamorphic transformation of ultramafic rock from the Earth's mantle.

ItemDataXML.Name: Polished Gabbro Brick
ItemDataXML.Desc: Gabbro is a dark plutonic rock that is considered to be the plutonic equivalent of basalt. Unlike granite, gabbro is low in silica and has no quartz.

ItemDataXML.Name: Polished Granite Brick
ItemDataXML.Desc: Granite is a common and widely occurring type of intrusive igneous rock. Granite is nearly always massive (lacking internal structures), hard and tough, and therefore it has gained widespread use as a construction stone.

ItemDataXML.Name: Polished Komatiite Brick
ItemDataXML.Desc: Komatiite is a type of ultramafic mantle-derived volcanic rock. True komatiites are very rare and essentially restricted to rocks of Archaean age. Komatiite magma is extremely dense and unlikely to reach the surface, being more likely to pool lower within the crust.

ItemDataXML.Name: Polished Marble Brick
ItemDataXML.Desc: Marble is a metamorphic rock composed of carbonate minerals, most commonly calcite or dolomite. Marble is commonly used for sculpture and as a building material.

ItemDataXML.Name: Polished Rhyolite Brick
ItemDataXML.Desc: Rhyolite can be considered as the extrusive equivalent to the plutonic granite rock, and outcrops of rhyolite may bear a resemblance to granite. Rhyolites that cool too quickly to grow crystals form a natural glass or vitrophyre, also called obsidian.

ItemDataXML.Name: Polished Greenstone Brick
ItemDataXML.Desc: Greenstone is several types of hard, durable and highly valued nephrite jade and bowenite found in New Zealand. It is used to make jewelry and weapons in the form of clubs.

ItemDataXML.Name: Console
ItemDataXML.Desc: A computer console that creates a soft glow in the room.

ItemDataXML.Name: Tech Panel
ItemDataXML.Desc: A panel full of high-tech... technology. Wonder what all this stuff does?

ItemDataXML.Name: Tech Lamp
ItemDataXML.Desc: A futuristic light source thats a bit brighter than fire but dimmer than a torch.

ItemDataXML.Name: Tech Furnace
ItemDataXML.Desc: A high-tech furnace that emits a glow ever so slightly softer than that of an open fire. Wait, where do I put the items to cook...?

ItemDataXML.Name: Plasma Conduit
ItemDataXML.Desc: A futuristic conduit full of glowing plasma, softly lighting up any dim, futuristic corridors it finds itself in.

ItemDataXML.Name: Steel Vent
ItemDataXML.Desc: A vent cover made from solid steel. Mainly used for ventilation, but you could probably take the cover off and explore the vents. If someone actually made a vent system behind this block.

ItemDataXML.Name: Berry Bush
ItemDataXML.Desc: Wild Berries on a small bush. Has a chance to yield fruit when harvested.

ItemDataXML.Name: Smooth Gray
ItemDataXML.Desc: A smooth gray colored block used for construction and decoration.

ItemDataXML.Name: Blood Orange
ItemDataXML.Desc: A blood orange colored block used for construction and decoration.

ItemDataXML.Name: Color Lime
ItemDataXML.Desc: A lime colored block used for construction and decoration.

ItemDataXML.Name: Light Tan
ItemDataXML.Desc: A light tan colored block used for construction and decoration.

ItemDataXML.Name: Color Leather
ItemDataXML.Desc: A leather colored block used for construction and decoration.

ItemDataXML.Name: Light Orange
ItemDataXML.Desc: A light orange colored block used for construction and decoration.

ItemDataXML.Name: Light Yellow
ItemDataXML.Desc: A light yellow colored block used for construction and decoration.

ItemDataXML.Name: Light Blue
ItemDataXML.Desc: A light blue colored block used for construction and decoration.

ItemDataXML.Name: Dark Blue
ItemDataXML.Desc: A dark blue colored block used for construction and decoration.

ItemDataXML.Name: Light Green
ItemDataXML.Desc: A light green colored block used for construction and decoration.

ItemDataXML.Name: Dark Brown
ItemDataXML.Desc: A dark brown colored block used for construction and decoration.

ItemDataXML.Name: Tan
ItemDataXML.Desc: A tan colored block used for construction and decoration.

ItemDataXML.Name: Creme
ItemDataXML.Desc: A creme colored block used for construction and decoration.

ItemDataXML.Name: Dark Red
ItemDataXML.Desc: A dark red colored block used for construction and decoration.

ItemDataXML.Name: Dark Gray
ItemDataXML.Desc: A dark gray colored block used for construction and decoration.

ItemDataXML.Name: Gray
ItemDataXML.Desc: A gray colored block used for construction and decoration.

ItemDataXML.Name: Dark Green
ItemDataXML.Desc: A dark green colored block used for construction and decoration.

ItemDataXML.Name: Pink
ItemDataXML.Desc: A pink colored block used for construction and decoration.

ItemDataXML.Name: Purple
ItemDataXML.Desc: A purple colored block used for construction and decoration.

ItemDataXML.Name: Cyan
ItemDataXML.Desc: A cyan colored block used for construction and decoration.

ItemDataXML.Name: Black
ItemDataXML.Desc: A black colored block used for construction and decoration.

ItemDataXML.Name: White
ItemDataXML.Desc: A white colored block used for construction and decoration.

ItemDataXML.Name: Yellow
ItemDataXML.Desc: A yellow colored block used for construction and decoration.

ItemDataXML.Name: Red
ItemDataXML.Desc: A red colored block used for construction and decoration.

ItemDataXML.Name: Orange
ItemDataXML.Desc: An orange colored block used for construction and decoration.

ItemDataXML.Name: Light Brown
ItemDataXML.Desc: A light brown colored block used for construction and decoration.

ItemDataXML.Name: Brown
ItemDataXML.Desc: A brown colored block used for construction and decoration.

ItemDataXML.Name: Green
ItemDataXML.Desc: A green colored block used for construction and decoration.

ItemDataXML.Name: Blue
ItemDataXML.Desc: A blue colored block used for construction and decoration.

ItemDataXML.Name: Marker
ItemDataXML.Desc: X marks the spot! Markers are special blocks for use in creative mode. Do not use them for decoration! They should only be used as markers for fill/clear/replace/copy operations.

ItemDataXML.Name: Exclude Marker
ItemDataXML.Desc: Exclude Markers are special blocks for use in creative mode. Do not use them for decoration! They should only be used as exclusion markers for fill/clear/replace/copy operations.

ItemDataXML.Name: zLastBlockID
ItemDataXML.Desc: Modded Block

ItemDataXML.Name: Hand
ItemDataXML.Desc: Nice to meet you.

ItemDataXML.Name: Wood Pickaxe
ItemDataXML.Desc: This pickaxe is the most primitive. It can only mine the softer rock near the surface and will degrade quickly. It can not mine any precious ores.

ItemDataXML.Name: Iron Pickaxe
ItemDataXML.Desc: This pickaxe is primitive but far superior to the wood version. It can only mine the softer rock near the surface, but can also mine some precious ores. It will last much longer than a wood pickaxe.

ItemDataXML.Name: Steel Pickaxe
ItemDataXML.Desc: This pickaxe will bring you out of the stone age and into modern times. It can mine some of the harder rock found quite deep underground and most precious ores. It will last 2 - 3 times longer than an iron pickaxe.

ItemDataXML.Name: Diamond Pickaxe
ItemDataXML.Desc: This pickaxe is extremely hard and can mine most of the deeper hard rock and all precious ores. It will last twice as long as a steel pickaxe.

ItemDataXML.Name: Ruby Pickaxe
ItemDataXML.Desc: A diamond pickaxe at it's core, but the blade is tipped with ultra hard rubies. It is crafted from 1 diamond pickaxe and 2 ruby gemstones.

ItemDataXML.Name: Titanium Pickaxe
ItemDataXML.Desc: The ultimate pickaxe. A diamond pickaxe at it's core, but the blade is titanium tipped. You can mine to the bottom of the world with this puppy, and the bottom of the world is deep. It is crafted from 1 diamond pickaxe and 2 titanium carbide bars.

ItemDataXML.Name: Wood Hatchet
ItemDataXML.Desc: This hatchet is the most primitive. It is slow to cut trees and degrades quickly.

ItemDataXML.Name: Iron Hatchet
ItemDataXML.Desc: This hatchet is primitive but far superior to the wood version and will last much longer.

ItemDataXML.Name: Steel Hatchet
ItemDataXML.Desc: This hatchet will last 2 - 3 times longer than an iron hatchet.

ItemDataXML.Name: Diamond Hatchet
ItemDataXML.Desc: This is the ultimate hatchet. Extremely hard and will last twice as long as a steel hatchet.

ItemDataXML.Name: Wood Shovel
ItemDataXML.Desc: This shovel is the most primitive. It is slow to dig and degrades quickly.

ItemDataXML.Name: Iron Shovel
ItemDataXML.Desc: This shovel is primitive but far superior to the wood version and will last much longer.

ItemDataXML.Name: Steel Shovel
ItemDataXML.Desc: This shovel will last 2 - 3 times longer than an iron shovel.

ItemDataXML.Name: Diamond Shovel
ItemDataXML.Desc: This is the ultimate shovel. Extremely hard and will last twice as long as a steel shovel.

ItemDataXML.Name: Wood Spear
ItemDataXML.Desc: This spear is the most primitive. It is soft and degrades quickly.

ItemDataXML.Name: Iron Spear
ItemDataXML.Desc: This spear is primitive but superior to the bronze version and will last much longer.

ItemDataXML.Name: Steel Spear
ItemDataXML.Desc: This spear will last 2 - 3 times longer than an iron spear.

ItemDataXML.Name: Diamond Spear
ItemDataXML.Desc: This is the ultimate spear. Extremely hard and will last twice as long as a steel spear.

ItemDataXML.Name: Wood Sword
ItemDataXML.Desc: This sword is the most primitive. It is soft and degrades quickly.

ItemDataXML.Name: Iron Sword
ItemDataXML.Desc: This sword is primitive but superior to the bronze version and will last longer.

ItemDataXML.Name: Steel Sword
ItemDataXML.Desc: This sword will last 2 - 3 times longer than an iron sword.

ItemDataXML.Name: Diamond Sword
ItemDataXML.Desc: This is the ultimate sword. Extremely hard and will last twice as long as a steel sword.

ItemDataXML.Name: Binoculars
ItemDataXML.Desc: Binoculars use two lenses to magnify objects seen through them. When using Binoculars, press and hold the left and right shoulder buttons or use the scroll wheel to zoom the view.

ItemDataXML.Name: Camera
ItemDataXML.Desc: Smile!

ItemDataXML.Name: Stick
ItemDataXML.Desc: Sticks are used to craft many things from pickaxes to swords to ladders and torches.

ItemDataXML.Name: Iron Ingot
ItemDataXML.Desc: Iron bars are smelted from iron ores in the furnace and used to craft all types of iron equipment.

ItemDataXML.Name: Steel Ingot
ItemDataXML.Desc: Steel bars are smelted from iron bars and carbon in the furnace and used to craft all types of steel equipment.

ItemDataXML.Name: Gold Bar
ItemDataXML.Desc: Gold bars are smelted from gold ores in the furnace and used to feel like a king.

ItemDataXML.Name: Ruby Gem Stone
ItemDataXML.Desc: Ruby gemstones are extracted from ruby containing rock in the furnace. Used to make ruby-tipped diamond pickaxes and weapons.

ItemDataXML.Name: Diamond Gem Stone
ItemDataXML.Desc: Diamond gemstones are extracted from diamond containing rock in the furnace. Used to make all diamond equipment and also to look pretty.

ItemDataXML.Name: Gold Pieces
ItemDataXML.Desc: It's a rich mans world. How many chests full of gold can you accumulate?

ItemDataXML.Name: Titanium Ingot
ItemDataXML.Desc: An extremely hard titanium alloy. Fashioned in the furnace and used to craft the ultimate pickaxe - the titanium-tipped diamond pickaxe. And also titanium weapons.

ItemDataXML.Name: Grenade Launcher
ItemDataXML.Desc: Fire in the hole! An extremely violent but awesome weapon.

ItemDataXML.Name: Grenade
ItemDataXML.Desc: Ammunition for the grenade launcher.

ItemDataXML.Name: Raw Fish
ItemDataXML.Desc: A bit smelly. Not edible. Cook it in the furnace.

ItemDataXML.Name: Cooked Fish
ItemDataXML.Desc: Yum yum fresh fish! A health replenishment.

ItemDataXML.Name: Bucket
ItemDataXML.Desc: Buckets are good for carrying liquids

ItemDataXML.Name: Bucket Of Water
ItemDataXML.Desc: A bucket filled with water. Used to make ponds.

ItemDataXML.Name: Bucket Of Lava
ItemDataXML.Desc: A bucket filled with lava. Take care.

ItemDataXML.Name: Wood Shield
ItemDataXML.Desc: A primitive shield made from wood.

ItemDataXML.Name: Iron Shield
ItemDataXML.Desc: A useful shield made from iron.

ItemDataXML.Name: Steel Shield
ItemDataXML.Desc: A good shield made from high quality steel.

ItemDataXML.Name: Diamond Shield
ItemDataXML.Desc: An excellent shield crafted from the strongest of gemstones.

ItemDataXML.Name: Wood Door
ItemDataXML.Desc: Open... Close... Open... Close... Open... Close...

ItemDataXML.Name: Steel Door
ItemDataXML.Desc: Open... Close... Open... Close... Open... Close... A sturdy steel door with blast resistance.

ItemDataXML.Name: Sledge Hammer
ItemDataXML.Desc: The One Hit Wonder. A different approach to block destruction. Spiders can drop these, but only if you hit rock bottom.

ItemDataXML.Name: Sign
ItemDataXML.Desc: Don't you just hate all those advertisements?

ItemDataXML.Name: Lock
ItemDataXML.Desc: Locks can be added to chests and doors to make them secure.

ItemDataXML.Name: Wood Bow
ItemDataXML.Desc: A ranged weapon made from wood. Used by wimps to attack from afar.

ItemDataXML.Name: Flint Arrow
ItemDataXML.Desc: Primitive ammunition for ranged weapons.

ItemDataXML.Name: Feather
ItemDataXML.Desc: Light and fluffy. Probably once belonged to a duck. Good for creating ranged weapon ammunition.

ItemDataXML.Name: Flint Flake
ItemDataXML.Desc: Flakes from Flint rock. Can be used to create arrow tips.

ItemDataXML.Name: Raw Lamb Chops
ItemDataXML.Desc: Raw meat from a younger sheep. Cook it to make it edible. Come on now, chop chop!

ItemDataXML.Name: Cooked Lamb Chops
ItemDataXML.Desc: A cut of lamb, cooked to perfection.

ItemDataXML.Name: Raw Duck Meat
ItemDataXML.Desc: The meat of a duck, unhealthy to eat raw. Why do ducks squeak like that? Is it made from rubber or something?

ItemDataXML.Name: Cooked Duck Meat
ItemDataXML.Desc: Cooking the duck meat proves it is not only not rubber, but delicious.

ItemDataXML.Name: Bed
ItemDataXML.Desc: ZZZZZZzzzzzzzzzz. Sleep tight, don't let the bed bugs bite. If you're afraid of night, beds will help your plight.

ItemDataXML.Name: Locked Door
ItemDataXML.Desc: Open... Close... Open... Close... Open... Close... Oh wait, it's locked!

ItemDataXML.Name: Lighter
ItemDataXML.Desc: Click, click, click, WHOOOOOOSH!

ItemDataXML.Name: Iron Arrow
ItemDataXML.Desc: Basic ammunition for ranged weapons.

ItemDataXML.Name: Steel Arrow
ItemDataXML.Desc: Good ammunition for ranged weapons.

ItemDataXML.Name: Diamond Arrow
ItemDataXML.Desc: Excellent ammunition for ranged weapons.

ItemDataXML.Name: Ruby Arrow
ItemDataXML.Desc: Superior ammunition for ranged weapons.

ItemDataXML.Name: Titanium Arrow
ItemDataXML.Desc: The ultimate ammunition for ranged weapons.

ItemDataXML.Name: Boom Arrow
ItemDataXML.Desc: Explosive ammunition for ranged weapons.

ItemDataXML.Name: Fire Arrow
ItemDataXML.Desc: Hazardous ammunition for ranged weapons.

ItemDataXML.Name: Clipboard
ItemDataXML.Desc: Used for copying and pasting sections of your world.

ItemDataXML.Name: Fence
ItemDataXML.Desc: What a lovely fence! Let's keep the riff-raff out!

ItemDataXML.Name: Rope
ItemDataXML.Desc: Rope is more convenient than ladders for deep shafts. Place rope at the top, and it will uncoil downwards until it hits something or it's length runs out. Rope ascend and descend speed is quicker than ladders. Rope is also ideal to descend open spaces as it does not need to be attached to a solid block.

ItemDataXML.Name: Stairs
ItemDataXML.Desc: Up... Down... Up... Down... Up... Down...Nice trip?

ItemDataXML.Name: Half Block
ItemDataXML.Desc: Half of a block.

ItemDataXML.Name: Ramp
ItemDataXML.Desc: A sloped ramp block.

ItemDataXML.Name: Cylinder
ItemDataXML.Desc: A cylinder shaped block.

ItemDataXML.Name: Skeleton Key
ItemDataXML.Desc: A master key to unlock anything.

ItemDataXML.Name: Skull Key
ItemDataXML.Desc: A key to unlock things.

ItemDataXML.Name: Silk Key
ItemDataXML.Desc: A key to unlock things.

ItemDataXML.Name: Diamond Key
ItemDataXML.Desc: A key to unlock things.

ItemDataXML.Name: Titanium Key
ItemDataXML.Desc: A key to unlock things.

ItemDataXML.Name: Gold Key
ItemDataXML.Desc: A key to unlock things.

ItemDataXML.Name: Shadow Key
ItemDataXML.Desc: A key to unlock things.

ItemDataXML.Name: Dwarf Key
ItemDataXML.Desc: A key to unlock things.

ItemDataXML.Name: Dark Key
ItemDataXML.Desc: A key to unlock things.

ItemDataXML.Name: Key Of Light
ItemDataXML.Desc: A key to unlock things.

ItemDataXML.Name: Bob Key
ItemDataXML.Desc: A key to unlock things.

ItemDataXML.Name: Stone Key
ItemDataXML.Desc: A key to unlock things.

ItemDataXML.Name: Ghost Key
ItemDataXML.Desc: A key to unlock things.

ItemDataXML.Name: Titan Key
ItemDataXML.Desc: A key to unlock things.

ItemDataXML.Name: Hero Key
ItemDataXML.Desc: A key to unlock things.

ItemDataXML.Name: Lightning Key
ItemDataXML.Desc: A key to unlock things.

ItemDataXML.Name: Luna Key
ItemDataXML.Desc: A key to unlock things.

ItemDataXML.Name: Solar Key
ItemDataXML.Desc: A key to unlock things.

ItemDataXML.Name: Celestial Key
ItemDataXML.Desc: A key to unlock things.

ItemDataXML.Name: Slime Key
ItemDataXML.Desc: A key to unlock things.

ItemDataXML.Name: Nature Key
ItemDataXML.Desc: A key to unlock things.

ItemDataXML.Name: Undead Key
ItemDataXML.Desc: A key to unlock things.

ItemDataXML.Name: Spider Key
ItemDataXML.Desc: A key to unlock things.

ItemDataXML.Name: Fish Key
ItemDataXML.Desc: A key to unlock things.

ItemDataXML.Name: Ruby Key
ItemDataXML.Desc: A key to unlock things.

ItemDataXML.Name: Nautical Key
ItemDataXML.Desc: A key to unlock things.

ItemDataXML.Name: Teleport Key
ItemDataXML.Desc: A key to unlock things.

ItemDataXML.Name: Battle Axe
ItemDataXML.Desc: A powerful combat Axe. This axe can inflict serious damage and gives serious stat bonuses to boot.

ItemDataXML.Name: Elven Bow
ItemDataXML.Desc: A powerful bow crafted by ancient Elves. This bow can shoot arrows without depleting your quiver. It also has the fastest shot speed of any known bow and gives serious stat bonuses.

ItemDataXML.Name: Amulet Of Flight
ItemDataXML.Desc: An amulet that grants you the power of flight.

ItemDataXML.Name: Water Talisman
ItemDataXML.Desc: A talisman that grants you the ability to breathe under water.

ItemDataXML.Name: Shield Badge
ItemDataXML.Desc: A special badge of protection.

ItemDataXML.Name: Ten League Boots
ItemDataXML.Desc: Gravity defying Super Boots.

ItemDataXML.Name: Table
ItemDataXML.Desc: A useful indoor decoration. Now, go set the table, we're expecting guests over for dinner tonight.

ItemDataXML.Name: Switch
ItemDataXML.Desc: A switch that delivers power to adjacent blocks.

ItemDataXML.Name: Button
ItemDataXML.Desc: Press... Pop... Press... Pop... Press... Pop... Press... Pop... Press... Pop...

ItemDataXML.Name: Iron Hoe
ItemDataXML.Desc: Use this tool on grass and dirt to till it for growing crops. Don't forget to irrigate the tilled earth!

ItemDataXML.Name: Steel Hoe
ItemDataXML.Desc: A reasonably good Hoe.

ItemDataXML.Name: Diamond Hoe
ItemDataXML.Desc: A strong and efficient Hoe.

ItemDataXML.Name: Iron Scythe
ItemDataXML.Desc: Use this tool to harvest your crops. It is slow and doesn't give the best harvest but it's good for novice farmers. Try not to harvest crops before they've fully grown, otherwise a poor harvest ye will reap.

ItemDataXML.Name: Steel Scythe
ItemDataXML.Desc: Steel Scythe. Reaps a better and quicker harvest than an Iron Scythe, but still not the best.

ItemDataXML.Name: Diamond Scythe
ItemDataXML.Desc: Diamond Scythe. Nasty looking thing. Reaps a good harvest though, and doesn't take all day.

ItemDataXML.Name: Wheat Seed
ItemDataXML.Desc: Plant this seed onto irrigated tilled earth to grow Wheat crops.

ItemDataXML.Name: Sugar Cane Seed
ItemDataXML.Desc: Plant this seed onto irrigated tilled earth to grow Sugar Cane.

ItemDataXML.Name: Wheat
ItemDataXML.Desc: Flour is crafted from Wheat. Dough is crafted from Flour. Bread is baked from Dough.

ItemDataXML.Name: Sugar
ItemDataXML.Desc: The greatest ingredient known to man.

ItemDataXML.Name: Egg
ItemDataXML.Desc: Egg. There are good eggs, and bad eggs, and in between good and bad eggs and in between good and bad but leaning towards bad eggs, and in between good and bad but leaning towards good eggs and... hmm nevermind.

ItemDataXML.Name: Flour
ItemDataXML.Desc: Flour. Ground from wheat, usually.

ItemDataXML.Name: Bottle
ItemDataXML.Desc: Bottle. Transparent containment genius.

ItemDataXML.Name: Bottle Of Water
ItemDataXML.Desc: A bottle filled with water.

ItemDataXML.Name: Bottle Of Milk
ItemDataXML.Desc: A bottle filled with milk.

ItemDataXML.Name: Bucket Of Milk
ItemDataXML.Desc: A bucket filled with milk. How did you get this? You probably swung an empty bucket at a cow?

ItemDataXML.Name: Dough
ItemDataXML.Desc: Doh!

ItemDataXML.Name: Bread
ItemDataXML.Desc: Bread. Fundamental food for like ever.

ItemDataXML.Name: Cake
ItemDataXML.Desc: Cake. And eat it too (doh).

ItemDataXML.Name: Butter
ItemDataXML.Desc: Butter. Apply thick and don't scimp on the edges.

ItemDataXML.Name: Cheese
ItemDataXML.Desc: Cheese. Dairy goodness. Some cheeses bring water to the eyes, some induce vomiting, while others taste like heaven.

ItemDataXML.Name: Salt
ItemDataXML.Desc: Too much salt can kill you, too little and our food doesn't taste as good. I cannot eat tomatoes without salt.

ItemDataXML.Name: Pizza
ItemDataXML.Desc: Pizza. Is Pizza healthy? Who cares right? Sure does taste good.

ItemDataXML.Name: Tomato
ItemDataXML.Desc: Tomato. I think we know what these are. Oh wait, did you know Tomato is a fruit? If you eat a salad of tomatoes, avacado, capsicum or chilli peppers, you are in fact eating a fruit salad. Yes, I kid you not, really, it's true, I think...

ItemDataXML.Name: Iron Battle Axe
ItemDataXML.Desc: An Iron Battle Axe. What more is there to say.

ItemDataXML.Name: Steel Battle Axe
ItemDataXML.Desc: A mid level Steel Battle Axe. A strong and trusty weapon for the thick armed melee warrior.

ItemDataXML.Name: Greenstone Gold Battle Axe
ItemDataXML.Desc: A mid level Greenstone Battle Axe with Gold trim. More likely to bludgeon than slash, but who cares, only the result matters right?

ItemDataXML.Name: Diamond Battle Axe
ItemDataXML.Desc: A mid to high level Battle Axe, forged from Diamond. Shiny, Sharp, Strong and Sexy.

ItemDataXML.Name: Ruby Battle Axe
ItemDataXML.Desc: A Ruby Tipped Diamond Battle Axe. A high level Battle Axe. Feed the ruby tips with your foes blood, and they'll shine brighter.

ItemDataXML.Name: Titanium Battle Axe
ItemDataXML.Desc: The ultimate Battle Axe. Your foes will turn and run, or at least they should. If they don't, they're dumb, and probably dead, by now.

ItemDataXML.Name: Platinum Sword
ItemDataXML.Desc: A Platinum Sword with Platinum Strength and Platinum Shine. Doesn't give air points though.

ItemDataXML.Name: Ruby Sword
ItemDataXML.Desc: A Ruby Tipped Diamond Sword. Better than a Diamond Sword, naturally. Prettier too. Blood from your enemies will make the tips glow brighter. Feed them!

ItemDataXML.Name: Titanium Sword
ItemDataXML.Desc: The ulitmate sword with the ultimate looks, and the ultimate price. This distinctively familiar design will strike fear into the hearts of beasts.

ItemDataXML.Name: Steel Scimitar
ItemDataXML.Desc: A Steel Scimitar. Why am I hearing medieval Arab music in the background?

ItemDataXML.Name: Steel Pike
ItemDataXML.Desc: A Steel Pike. Long and pointy, does more puncturing than slicing. This sword provides Defence XP rather than Attack XP.

ItemDataXML.Name: Steel Claymore
ItemDataXML.Desc: A Steel Claymore. Big and brash, does more crushing than cutting. This sword provides Strength XP rather than Attack XP.

ItemDataXML.Name: Steel Katana
ItemDataXML.Desc: A Steel Katana. Got a Samurai fetish? Then this sword is for you.

ItemDataXML.Name: Greenstone Gold Pickaxe
ItemDataXML.Desc: A mid level, and beautiful looking, pickaxe. Better than steel, not as good as diamond.

ItemDataXML.Name: Greenstone Gold Hatchet
ItemDataXML.Desc: A mid level, and beautiful looking, hatchet. Better than steel, not as good as diamond.

ItemDataXML.Name: Greenstone Gold Shovel
ItemDataXML.Desc: Why would anybody dig dirt with a shovel as beautiful as this? Be sure to polish it after you're done digging.

ItemDataXML.Name: Greenstone Gold Sword
ItemDataXML.Desc: A mid level sword. As hard and sharp as stone but as beautiful as jewelry. Which is more important, damaging your enemy, or looking good while trying. You decide.

ItemDataXML.Name: Greenstone Gold Shield
ItemDataXML.Desc: A great shield crafted intricately from Greenstone and Gold. Which is more important, defending yourself, or looking good while dieing. You decide.

ItemDataXML.Name: Greenstone Gold Sledge Hammer
ItemDataXML.Desc: The One Hit Wonder with a green and gold hue.

ItemDataXML.Name: Tomato Seed
ItemDataXML.Desc: Plant this seed onto irrigated tilled earth to grow Tomato Plants.

ItemDataXML.Name: Raw Beef
ItemDataXML.Desc: Raw Beef. I could eat this, but I think it would taste a lot nicer if cooked.

ItemDataXML.Name: Cooked Beef
ItemDataXML.Desc: Cooked Beef. Is ignorance bliss? If we are ignorant of the fact that we are ignorant, then probably yes, otherwise no, it irks.

ItemDataXML.Name: Cow Hide
ItemDataXML.Desc: Raw Leather. Can be crafted/tanned into leather which can in turn be used to make things such as armor.

ItemDataXML.Name: Leather
ItemDataXML.Desc: Tanned Cow Hide. Useful for making such things as armor.

ItemDataXML.Name: Obsidian Gem Stone
ItemDataXML.Desc: A pure obsidian jewel, extracted from an obsidian block. Can be used to enchant jewelry.

ItemDataXML.Name: Ring Mould
ItemDataXML.Desc: Used to craft rings.

ItemDataXML.Name: Amulet Mould
ItemDataXML.Desc: Used to craft amulets.

ItemDataXML.Name: Necklace Mould
ItemDataXML.Desc: Used to craft necklaces.

ItemDataXML.Name: Gold Ring
ItemDataXML.Desc: A beautiful gold ring. Enchant it with other items to give it power.

ItemDataXML.Name: Gold Amulet
ItemDataXML.Desc: A beautiful gold amulet. Enchant it with other items to give it power.

ItemDataXML.Name: Gold Necklace
ItemDataXML.Desc: A beautiful gold necklace. Enchant it with other items to give it power.

ItemDataXML.Name: Ring Of Bob
ItemDataXML.Desc: An Enchanted Greenstone Ring. 10% of any damage you receive from an enemy attack will be recoiled back on to the enemy.

ItemDataXML.Name: Amulet Of Fury
ItemDataXML.Desc: An Enchanted Greenstone Amulet. Increases your chance of inflicting a max hit on your opponent during combat by 15%

ItemDataXML.Name: Necklace Of Knowledge
ItemDataXML.Desc: An Enchanted Greenstone Necklace. All XP earned is increased by 20%

ItemDataXML.Name: Spider Ring
ItemDataXML.Desc: An Enchanted Ruby Ring. Grants the ability to walk up walls. It also reduces fall damage and allows the wearer to walk over steel spikes without harm. Negative Combat bonuses are in effect while equipped.

ItemDataXML.Name: Predator Amulet
ItemDataXML.Desc: An Enchanted Ruby Amulet. You become a silhouette. Mobs cannot see you unless you are in combat with them. Mobs can still hear you though.

ItemDataXML.Name: Necklace Of Hypocrisy
ItemDataXML.Desc: An Enchanted Ruby Necklace. When mining naturally generated ores, this necklace gives you an 80% chance of harvesting two ores from the one block, and a 20% chance you'll get none.

ItemDataXML.Name: Ring Of Exemption
ItemDataXML.Desc: An Enchanted Diamond Ring. If your health becomes critical (less than 15% of your Max Health), you will be instantly teleported to the closest spawn point.

ItemDataXML.Name: Amulet Of Starlight
ItemDataXML.Desc: An Enchanted Diamond Amulet that will surround you in brilliant star light while equipped.

ItemDataXML.Name: Necklace Of Farsight
ItemDataXML.Desc: An Enchanted Diamond Necklace. Grants you the power to see through solid rock. But beware, you will take 5 hit points of damage every second and your combat stats receive a massive negative bonus. You also move very slowly.

ItemDataXML.Name: Diamantium Ingot
ItemDataXML.Desc: Diamantium bars are forged from steel and diamond and used to craft many types of equipment.

ItemDataXML.Name: Diamantium Sword
ItemDataXML.Desc: A big heavy beast of a sword. Forged from super hard diamantium. This sword will make any warrior very dangerous.

ItemDataXML.Name: Coif
ItemDataXML.Desc: Low level armor for ranged warriors. Coifs offer less restricted vision than the leather helmet, making them better for rangers.

ItemDataXML.Name: Leather Body
ItemDataXML.Desc: Low level armor for both ranged and melee warriors. Gives a small ranged bonus.

ItemDataXML.Name: Leather Leggings
ItemDataXML.Desc: Low level armor for both ranged and melee warriors. Gives a small ranged bonus.

ItemDataXML.Name: Leather Helmet
ItemDataXML.Desc: Low level armor for both ranged and melee warriors. Gives a small ranged bonus.

ItemDataXML.Name: Leather Boots
ItemDataXML.Desc: Low level armor for both ranged and melee warriors. Gives a small ranged bonus.

ItemDataXML.Name: Leather Gauntlets
ItemDataXML.Desc: Low level armor for both ranged and melee warriors. Gives a small ranged bonus.

ItemDataXML.Name: Iron Body
ItemDataXML.Desc: Low level armor for melee warriors.

ItemDataXML.Name: Iron Leggings
ItemDataXML.Desc: Low level armor for melee warriors.

ItemDataXML.Name: Iron Helmet
ItemDataXML.Desc: Low level armor for melee warriors.

ItemDataXML.Name: Iron Boots
ItemDataXML.Desc: Low level armor for melee warriors.

ItemDataXML.Name: Iron Gauntlets
ItemDataXML.Desc: Low level armor for melee warriors.

ItemDataXML.Name: Steel Body
ItemDataXML.Desc: Mid level armor for melee warriors.

ItemDataXML.Name: Steel Leggings
ItemDataXML.Desc: Mid level armor for melee warriors.

ItemDataXML.Name: Steel Helmet
ItemDataXML.Desc: Mid level armor for melee warriors.

ItemDataXML.Name: Steel Boots
ItemDataXML.Desc: Mid level armor for melee warriors.

ItemDataXML.Name: Steel Gauntlets
ItemDataXML.Desc: Mid level armor for melee warriors.

ItemDataXML.Name: Diamantium Body
ItemDataXML.Desc: Mid to high level armor. Forged from super hard Diamantium. Any melee warrior would be happy with this fine armor.

ItemDataXML.Name: Diamantium Leggings
ItemDataXML.Desc: Mid to high level armor. Forged from super hard Diamantium. Any melee warrior would be happy with this fine armor.

ItemDataXML.Name: Diamantium Helmet
ItemDataXML.Desc: Mid to high level armor. Forged from super hard Diamantium. Any melee warrior would be happy with this fine armor.

ItemDataXML.Name: Diamantium Boots
ItemDataXML.Desc: Mid to high level armor. Forged from super hard Diamantium. Any melee warrior would be happy with this fine armor.

ItemDataXML.Name: Diamantium Gauntlets
ItemDataXML.Desc: Mid to high level armor. Forged from super hard Diamantium. Any melee warrior would be happy with this fine armor.

ItemDataXML.Name: Titanium Body
ItemDataXML.Desc: Ultimate armor for the ultimate melee warrior.

ItemDataXML.Name: Titanium Leggings
ItemDataXML.Desc: Ultimate armor for the ultimate melee warrior.

ItemDataXML.Name: Titanium Helmet
ItemDataXML.Desc: Ultimate armor for the ultimate melee warrior.

ItemDataXML.Name: Titanium Boots
ItemDataXML.Desc: Ultimate armor for the ultimate melee warrior.

ItemDataXML.Name: Titanium Gauntlets
ItemDataXML.Desc: Ultimate armor for the ultimate melee warrior.

ItemDataXML.Name: Diamantium Shield
ItemDataXML.Desc: A superior shield, forged from super hard Diamantium. Any melee warrior would be happy with this fine shield.

ItemDataXML.Name: Titanium Shield
ItemDataXML.Desc: The ultimate shield for the ultimate melee warrior.
